chapter_context.summary: Athaliah usurps Judah's throne and murders the royal seed, but young Joash is hidden in the Temple; Jehoiada the priest leads a coup, executes Athaliah, and crowns Joash king.

outline.text: Athaliah Massacres Royal Family of Judah; Joash Hidden in Temple
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Athaliah Massacres Royal Family of Judah; Joash Hidden in Temple (11:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehoiada the Priest Crowns Seven-Year-Old Joash King of Judah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehoiada the Priest Crowns Seven-Year-Old Joash King of Judah (11:4-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.
This section focuses on Athaliah Executed Near the Palace (11:13-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehoiada Renews Covenants; Baal's House Destroyed; Joash Reigns
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehoiada Renews Covenants; Baal's House Destroyed; Joash Reigns (11:17-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 11 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
